compiled_state.title: As a plugin operator, script file comments accurately describe the plugin without hardcoded install paths
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace header comments `# plugins/dso/scripts/foo.sh` with `# foo.sh` (filename only) and reword all shim-exempt marker comments that include the hardcoded path, in all .sh and .py files within plugins/dso/.
**Why**: Hardcoded install paths in comments mislead host project maintainers into thinking the plugin must live at plugins/dso/. Comments have no runtime effect but affect readability and trust.
**Scope**:
- IN: All lines in .sh and .py files under plugins/dso/ where plugins/dso appears in a comment (# prefixed or inline # comment)
- OUT: Runtime path construction lines in .sh scripts (covered by script runtime path story). Non-comment occurrences remain after this story closes.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all `# plugins/dso/...` header comment patterns in .sh and .py files under plugins/dso/ are replaced with short-form equivalents (e.g., `# foo.sh`), confirmed by: `grep -rn "# .*plugins/dso" plugins/dso/ --include="*.sh" --include="*.py"` returns zero matches
  ← Satisfies: "grep -r 'plugins/dso' plugins/dso/ returns zero matches"
- When this story is complete, shim-exempt marker comments that include plugins/dso/ in their justification text are reworded to describe the reason without naming the install path
  ← Satisfies: "grep -r 'plugins/dso' plugins/dso/ returns zero matches"
- When this story is complete, the verification grep specifically targets comment-type lines only — non-comment occurrences are explicitly out of scope and covered by the script runtime path story
  ← Satisfies: "grep -r 'plugins/dso' plugins/dso/ returns zero matches"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Scope boundary] The done-definition grep must match only comment lines. Run verification as `grep -rn "# .*plugins/dso"` (with `# ` prefix) to target comments specifically. Non-comment occurrences are S2's responsibility.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
